# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write Linkerd Server and ServerAuthorization resources for microservices. Define which services can communicate with each other. Configure port-based and service account-based authorization rules. This is for our zero-trust service mesh.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent linkerd authz missing.
# Category: linkerd_authz_missing

apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata: 
 name: default   # replace with your namespace if you have one, otherwise leave it as 'default' or remove this line altogether for the global service account in kubernetes cluster.   
namespace: linkerd-system     # optional - change to match where Linkerd is deployed (e.g., prod) 
---  
apiVersion: v1
kind: ServiceAccount
metadata: 
 name: server      # replace with your namespace if you have one, otherwise leave it as 'default' or remove this line altogether for the global service account in kubernetes cluster.   
namespace: linkerd-system     # optional - change to match where Linkerd is deployed (e.g., prod) 
---

kind: ServiceAccount
metadata: 
 name: serverauthorization      # replace with your namespace if you have one, otherwise leave it as 'default' or remove this line altogether for the global service account in kubernetes cluster.   
namespace: linkerd-system     # optional - change to match where Linkerd is deployed (e.g., prod) 
---  
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
 name: default      # replace with your namespace if you have one, otherwise leave it as 'default' or remove this line altogether for the global secret in kubernetes cluster.   
namespace: linkerd-system     # optional - change to match where Linkerd is deployed (e.g., prod) 
type: Opaque      # replace with your namespace if you have one, otherwise leave it as 'default' or remove this line altogether for the global secret type in kubernetes cluster  
data:      
 username: <base64-encoded-username>     # provide base64 encoded string of a user (e.g., "YWRtaW4=") 
 password: <base64-encoded-password>  # replace with your namespace if you have one, otherwise leave it as 'default' or remove this line altogether for the global secret in kubernetes cluster  
---   
apiVersion: v1     
kind: Secret      
metadata:        
 name: server     # provide a unique string to identify each resource.  This is used by Linkerd and must be globally unique within your K8s namespace or you'll get an error when trying to create it  
namespace: linkerd-system    # optional - change to match where Linkerd is deployed (e.g., prod)    
type: Opaque      # replace with the appropriate secret type for this resource  e.g, kubernetes/serviceaccount or secrets etc...      
data:        
 token: <base64-encoded-token>  # provide base64 encoded string of a service account (e.g., "YWRtaW4=")  
---   
apiVersion: v1     
kind: Secret       
metadata:         
 name: serverauthorization     # replace with your namespace if you have one, otherwise leave it as 'default' or remove this line altogether for the global secret in kubernetes cluster.  This is used by Linkerd and must be globally unique within your K8s namespace  
namespace: linkerd-system    # optional - change to match where Linkerd is deployed (e.g., prod)    
type: Opaque      # replace with the appropriate secret type for this resource  e.g, kubernetes/serviceaccount or secrets etc...      
data:        
 token: <base64-encoded-token>  # provide base64 encoded string of a service account (e.g., "YWRtaW4=")  
---   
apiVersion: linkerd.io/v1alpha2 
kind: LinkerdServiceAuthorization    
metadata:         
 name: default      # replace with your namespace if you have one, otherwise leave it as 'default' or remove this line altogether for the global resource in kubernetes cluster  
namespace: linkerd-system    # optional - change to match where Linkerd is deployed (e.g., prod)    
spec:     
 rules:      
  - action: Allow       
    source: SelectFrom(User("default"))         
---        
apiVersion: v1  
kind: Service          
metadata:             
 name: default-http             # replace with your namespace if you have one, otherwise leave it as 'default' or remove this line altogether for the global service in kubernetes cluster.   
namespace: linkerd-system     # optional - change to match where Linkerd is deployed (e.g., prod)  
spec:     
 type: LoadBalancer      
 ports:         
  port: <port>             # replace with your specific service's required Port number, e.g.:80   
 selector:          
  app: default-http         # selectors are used to match pods and services in Kubernetes  
---     
apiVersion: v1    
kind: Service         
metadata:             
 name: server             # replace with your namespace if you have one, otherwise leave it as 'default' or remove this line altogether for the global service in kubernetes cluster.   
namespace: linkerd-system   # optional - change to match where Linkerd is deployed (e.g., prod)    
spec:     
 type: LoadBalancer      
 ports:         
  port: <port>             # replace with your specific service's required Port number, e.g.:80   
 selector:          
  app: server              # selectors are used to match pods and services in Kubernetes  
---

spec:     
 type: LoadBalancer
